bundles.title: Lead Capture & Qualification

idealizedSteps.label: Generate standardized lead payload from vendor data
Lookup project type from customer selection
Lookup marketing sources for attribution
Build final contact payload with enriched data
Create or update contact in CRM
Build final deal payload with contact association
Create or update deal in CRM
Handle deal creation errors gracefully
Fetch deal properties from CRM
Extract deal properties for processing
Extract deal details for routing
Route to booking flow based on deal stage
Fetch contact properties from CRM
Extract contact properties for processing
Loop over associated deals for sync
Wait before processing engagements
Loop over call and meeting engagements

bundles.pain: Sales reps manually copying form submissions into the CRM, missing hot leads during evenings and weekends, inconsistent follow-up timing costing deals
bundles.solution: Every lead is captured instantly from any marketing channel, scored automatically, welcomed within 60 seconds, and routed to the right salesperson — even at 2am on a Sunday

bundles.title: Appointment Booking & Scheduling

idealizedSteps.label: Route booking data based on source
Select booking payload from routing decision
Retrieve owner assignment from database
Build initial deal payload with appointment data
Lookup project type from booking details
Lookup marketing sources for attribution
Extract customer email from booking
Prepare contact sync payload for CRM
Check if contact is searchable
Select contact result from search
Build array of project types for deal
Check deal stage for routing
Route to appropriate pipeline based on deal stage
Process cancellation data
Search for appointment in CRM
Update deal and calendar with cancellation
Validate reschedule booking data

bundles.pain: Phone tag with customers trying to schedule estimates, double-booked calendars, no-shows because reminders were forgotten, and hours lost to manual calendar management
bundles.solution: Customers book their own appointments from a personalized link, get automatic confirmations and reminders, and the whole team stays in sync without a single phone call
inefficiencies: Complex flow could be simplified (15+ steps)
Custom code could be replaced with native actions

bundles.title: Estimate-to-Deal Pipeline

idealizedSteps.label: Create estimate in estimating tool after site visit
Sync estimate details to HubSpot deal
Apply standardized deal naming convention
Update deal stage and pipeline position
Link project photos from site visit
Calculate profit margins and flag outliers
Notify sales manager of high-value estimates
Send estimate to customer for review

bundles.pain: Estimates sitting in one tool while deals live in another, sales managers manually copying numbers between systems, inconsistent deal naming making pipeline reports useless
bundles.solution: The moment an estimate is created, every system updates automatically — the CRM deal is created, named consistently, and the sales team sees real-time pipeline value without touching a spreadsheet

bundles.title: Change Order Management

idealizedSteps.label: Customer requests scope change on existing project
Create change order record linked to original deal
Calculate revised pricing and timeline impact
Apply change order label and categorization
Update deal value in CRM with new total
Send revised estimate to customer for approval
Update project timeline and crew schedule
Notify production team of scope changes

bundles.pain: Change orders scribbled on paper, forgotten price adjustments that eat into margins, crew showing up without knowing the scope changed, and deals stuck showing the wrong value for weeks
bundles.solution: Every change order flows through automatically — pricing updates, the CRM reflects the real deal value, and the production team knows about scope changes before they arrive on site

bundles.title: Contract Lifecycle

idealizedSteps.label: Generate contract from approved estimate
Send contract to customer for e-signature
Track contract status and send reminders
Customer signs contract electronically
Update deal stage to "Contract Signed" in CRM
Create project addendums if scope includes extras
Trigger job setup workflow automatically
Notify operations team that new job is confirmed

bundles.pain: Contracts emailed as PDFs, customers printing and scanning to sign, nobody knowing when a contract was actually signed until someone checks email, and jobs delayed waiting for paperwork
bundles.solution: Contracts go out with one click, customers sign on their phone, and the moment ink hits the digital page the entire back office starts spinning up the job — zero waiting, zero chasing

bundles.title: Deal-to-Job Processing

idealizedSteps.label: Deal marked as won in CRM
Register deal owner and assign project manager
Sync company and contact records across systems
Create job record in project management
Map HubSpot fields to project file structure
Generate job folder with all customer documents
Create initial production schedule
Notify crew leads of upcoming job

bundles.pain: Won deals sitting in the CRM while the ops team manually creates job records, copy-pasting customer info between three different systems, and new jobs falling through the cracks during busy season
bundles.solution: The moment a deal is won, every downstream system is populated automatically — job records, customer files, production schedules, and crew notifications happen without anyone lifting a finger

bundles.title: Production & Crew Tracking

idealizedSteps.label: Update daily production schedule from field data
Log time entries and crew hours for each job
Sync time tracking data to central database
Calculate daily production metrics per crew
Update project completion percentage
Flag jobs running behind schedule
Generate daily production summary for office
Alert project manager of schedule variances

bundles.pain: Office staff calling crews for updates, production data trapped in text messages and phone calls, no idea which jobs are on track until someone drives to the site, and weekly reports that are already outdated
bundles.solution: Field crews log production data once, and the entire office sees real-time progress — schedule variances trigger alerts automatically, and daily summaries write themselves

bundles.title: Time Tracking Sync Engine

idealizedSteps.label: Employees clock in and out via time tracking app
Sync job codes and locations to time system
Match time entries to active projects and jobs
Sync new and modified time logs to central database
Handle deleted or corrected time entries
Sync employee profiles and permissions
Deactivate job codes for completed projects
Generate payroll-ready time summaries

bundles.pain: Time entries in one app, job codes in another, payroll staff spending hours reconciling who worked where, deleted entries causing phantom hours, and new employees waiting days to be set up in the time system
bundles.solution: Every clock-in, correction, and deletion flows between systems in real-time — job codes stay current, employee profiles sync automatically, and payroll gets clean data without manual reconciliation

bundles.title: Invoice Lifecycle

idealizedSteps.label: Detect unbilled line items ready for invoicing
Create invoice in accounting software from project data
Sync invoice details back to project database
Send invoice to customer via email
Track payment status and sync updates
Handle invoice modifications and corrections
Process void requests and update all systems
Generate work order URLs for customer reference

bundles.pain: Unbilled work slipping through the cracks, invoices created in one system but invisible in another, customers getting outdated versions, and voided invoices still showing up in project dashboards
bundles.solution: Unbilled items are detected automatically, invoices flow between accounting and project management in real-time, and every modification syncs everywhere — nothing falls through the cracks
inefficiencies: Complex flow could be simplified (15+ steps)
Custom code could be replaced with native actions

bundles.title: Expense Management Pipeline
constituentProcesses: Marketing Vendor / Channel Lead Data Mapping

idealizedSteps.label: Import bills and expenses from accounting software
Match expenses to jobs and projects
Sync customer records between accounting and database
Link parent companies to job records
Link sub-customers to individual projects
Save expense attachments and receipts to database
Categorize transactions by type and department
Generate expense reports by job and time period

bundles.pain: Receipts in email, bills in accounting software, project costs in a spreadsheet — nobody can answer "how much did we spend on that job?" without an hour of digging through three different systems
bundles.solution: Every bill, receipt, and transaction is automatically matched to its job, categorized, and stored in one place — real-time job costing without the treasure hunt
inefficiencies: Complex flow could be simplified (15+ steps)
Custom code could be replaced with native actions

bundles.title: Reporting Dashboard Sync

idealizedSteps.label: Pull latest deal and revenue data from CRM
Aggregate metrics across all active pipelines
Calculate win rates, deal velocity, and revenue trends
Sync updated metrics to reporting spreadsheet
Refresh dashboard visualizations automatically
Flag significant changes or anomalies

bundles.pain: Sales managers manually pulling reports from the CRM every Monday, numbers that are already a week old by the time anyone sees them, and no early warning when pipeline health drops
bundles.solution: Dashboards refresh every two minutes with live CRM data — win rates, revenue trends, and pipeline health are always current, and anomalies trigger alerts before they become problems

bundles.title: Communication Hub

idealizedSteps.label: System event triggers outbound communication
Select communication channel based on event type
Send email notification with formatted details
Send SMS alert for time-sensitive updates
Log all sent messages to CRM activity timeline
Update estimate calendar with schedule changes
Route error notifications to technical team
Track delivery status and flag failures

bundles.pain: Important updates buried in someone's inbox, text messages sent manually that get forgotten during busy days, error notifications that nobody sees, and no record of what was communicated to whom
bundles.solution: Every system event automatically triggers the right communication on the right channel — emails, texts, calendar updates, and error alerts all fire without human intervention, with full audit trails in the CRM

bundles.title: HubSpot Contact Sync
constituentProcesses: Marketing Vendor / Channel Lead Data Mapping

idealizedSteps.label: Override contact payload with incoming data
Search for existing contact by ID in CRM
Search for existing contact by email in CRM
Select which search result to use
Merge incoming data with existing contact data
Route to create or update based on search result
Return final contact details for downstream use

bundles.pain: Sales reps manually searching for contacts before every call, duplicate records piling up in the CRM, and no consistent process for merging contact data from different marketing channels
bundles.solution: Every contact is automatically searched by ID and email, merged with existing data if found, or created fresh if new — zero duplicates, zero manual lookups, always the latest info
inefficiencies: Complex flow could be simplified (15+ steps)
Custom code could be replaced with native actions

bundles.title: Message Transpiler

idealizedSteps.label: Retrieve latest open deal for contact
Generate personalized booking link from deal context
Shorten booking URL for customer message
Route message to appropriate channel based on context

bundles.pain: Staff manually looking up deals, copying booking links, and shortening URLs before every customer message — slow, error-prone, and impossible to scale during peak season
bundles.solution: Every outbound customer message automatically pulls the latest deal context, generates a personalized booking link, shortens it, and routes the right message format — customers get the right link in seconds, not minutes
